text.title: Monthly Review Process and AI-Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Monthly CS review process, data collection setup, and agent-specific limitations for customer success metrics workflows.

section.title: Monthly Review Process
section.p: Monthly CS review steps: (1) review health distribution trends month-over-month, (2) identify cohort patterns (are specific signup cohorts churning faster?), (3) analyze at-risk segment root causes, (4) review expansion opportunity list from healthy accounts, (5) update playbooks based on learnings, (6) share insights with product and leadership.
section.reference: Monthly metrics tracking table: NPS, CSAT, TTV, adoption, expansion, NRR vs. targets

section.title: Data Collection Setup
section.p: Data source requirements by component: usage frequency (analytics platform event tracking — Mixpanel, Amplitude, Heap), feature adoption (analytics or feature-flag system), support sentiment (helpdesk ticket analysis — Zendesk, Intercom), engagement (email platform open/click data, CRM activity), payment health (billing platform — Stripe, Chargebee).

rule.statement: Fix telemetry before building a health score. Missing or malformed events produce scores that are meaningless and, worse, misleadingly confident. Spend the first iteration cycle validating event completeness against a sample of 20 accounts.
rule.rationale: A health score built on incomplete data produces random alert patterns. CSMs will notice immediately when the score does not match their direct experience, and trust in the system collapses — harder to rebuild than to avoid.

antipattern.description: LLMs over-credit qualitative sentiment ("user said they love it") and discount quantitative usage decay.
antipattern.reason: Weight quantitative facts explicitly in every health-score prompt. One positive support ticket does not offset 30 days of declining login frequency.

antipattern.description: Agent sends personalized customer-facing emails autonomously without human approval.
antipattern.reason: One mis-personalized "we noticed you stopped using X" to a power user who never stopped can permanently damage trust. Human approval is required for any CS outreach regardless of scoring confidence.

antipattern.description: Agent produces a single NPS sentiment verdict per ticket rather than a per-message score chain.
antipattern.reason: Single-verdict sentiment is uncalibratable. Require per-message 1–5 scores with the underlying text, so calibration drift can be detected and corrected.

antipattern.description: Health scores trained on currently-active customers underweight signals that drive early cohort churn.
antipattern.reason: Survivor bias: active accounts that made it to month 12 have systematically different feature-usage patterns than accounts that churned in month 2. Validate scores using churned accounts from T-90 days.

antipattern.description: Passing full event histories into LLM context for health analysis.
antipattern.reason: Token cost and context contamination. Pre-aggregate events to weekly usage buckets before any LLM call. Health scoring on raw event streams is an agent-economics anti-pattern.
